questions.prompt: What does the Existing Customer Budget Cap in Advantage+ Shopping Campaigns do?

choices.text: Specifies a CRM customer list and a percentage of campaign budget that may reach those existing customers. The rest goes to net-new acquisition. Lets ASC go broad (including retargeting overlap) while you control how much budget repaints existing buyers. Typical: 20-30% for established brands, 10-15% for new-acquisition focus.

questions.explanation: Cap controls retargeting-vs-new-acquisition split inside ASC.

questions.prompt: Advantage+ campaign with 2 creatives running 4 days, CPA way above manual. Diagnosis?

choices.text: Two reasons usually compound. (1) ASC's learning phase is longer than manual because it explores a wider audience — 4 days is too early to judge, wait 5-7 days. (2) ASC rotates aggressively across creatives; with only 2 it can't optimize variety. Add 5-10 more creatives (videos + statics + UGC) and let it run a full week before deciding. Pausing prematurely or operating with a thin creative library are the two most common ASC failure modes.

questions.explanation: Too early + too few creatives. ASC needs time and a library.
